title: argyranthemum pinnatifidum subsp. pinnatifidum
intro: Argyranthemum pinnatifidum subsp. pinnatifidum, also known as the Canary Island daisy, is a flowering plant native to the Canary Islands. It is a shrub-like perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is used for its health benefits, such as treating digestive issues, fever reduction, and skin conditions, due to its essential oils and flavonoids. Argyranthemum pinnatifidum subsp. pinnatifidum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in some regions.
